mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0% signals severe labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 7.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 trading days with a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during post-recession periods, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM growth surpasses 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to improved corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 95.0 index threshold indicates severe supply-side contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO breaches the 95.0 lower bound.
